pet.name: bb
pet.special_needs: Large senior dog with weight management needs, slower walks, joint support, and consistent hydration monitoring.
pet.characteristics: Golden Retrievers are large, active dogs requiring regular moderate exercise, consistent training, and mental enrichment. They thrive on routine and respond well to positive reinforcement. Standard care includes daily walks, play sessions, and swimming when available. Diet should be portion-controlled and high-quality, fed on a consistent schedule to support healthy weight. Regular grooming, ear cleaning, and dental care are essential. Joint health monitoring, appropriate bedding, and climate control become increasingly important with age. They are social dogs that benefit from regular interaction and do not tolerate prolonged isolation. Water access should be constant, and veterinary check-ups should occur annually or more frequently as the dog ages.
pet.species_profile_summary: Golden Retrievers typically live 10-12 years in captive/pet settings and require consistent exercise, weight management, joint support, and preventive health monitoring throughout their lifespan.

age_context.summary: Golden Retrievers typically live 10-12 years in captive/pet settings and require consistent exercise, weight management, joint support, and preventive health monitoring throughout their lifespan.

retrieved_passages.title: Puppy and Kitten Routine Basics
retrieved_passages.content: Exercise and enrichment for very young pets should stay short and positive. Puppies often do better with several brief walks or training moments instead of one long outing, and kittens benefit from short interactive play sessions that stop before exhaustion.

retrieved_passages.title: Enrichment and Daily Monitoring
retrieved_passages.content: Daily care plans are stronger when they combine essential care with realistic enrichment and observation. Short play, sniff walks, brushing, or calm interaction can improve quality of life without requiring large blocks of time.

retrieved_passages.title: Senior Pet Support
retrieved_passages.content: A senior pet plan should prioritize consistency over intensity. When time is limited, essential care such as feeding, hydration, medication reminders, and mobility support should be scheduled before optional enrichment.

retrieved_passages.title: Medication Routine and Safety
retrieved_passages.content: Medication-related tasks in a home routine should focus on reminders, documentation, and observation, not dosage changes. A planning assistant can remind the owner to give medication exactly as already prescribed and to log whether the dose was completed.

retrieved_passages.title: Enrichment and Daily Monitoring
retrieved_passages.content: When the day's schedule is tight, a planner should preserve high-priority essentials first and then fill remaining time with short enrichment or monitoring tasks that still fit the available budget.

raw_recommendations.name: Morning hydration check and water bowl refresh
raw_recommendations.notes: Ensure fresh water available and observe intake for senior joint support needs.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Senior dogs require consistent hydration monitoring as part of essential care.

raw_recommendations.name: Portion-controlled meal with weight management focus
raw_recommendations.notes: Feed high-quality, portion-controlled diet on consistent schedule for weight management.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Consistent feeding schedule supports healthy weight and routine for senior large breed.

raw_recommendations.name: Low-impact walk or sniff walk
raw_recommendations.notes: Slower-paced walk with joint support in mind; sniff walks provide enrichment without intensity.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Senior dogs benefit from consistent, low-intensity movement for joint health and routine.

raw_recommendations.name: Short calm interaction or gentle play session
raw_recommendations.notes: Brief, low-impact play or brushing to improve quality of life without exhaustion.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Short enrichment and monitoring tasks fit senior routine and support mental wellbeing.

raw_recommendations.name: Evening hydration and mobility observation
raw_recommendations.notes: Final water check and brief observation of movement and comfort for the day.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Consistent hydration and mobility monitoring are essential for senior joint support.

raw_recommendations.name: Indoor enrichment activity during rainy days
raw_recommendations.notes: Puzzle toys, scent games, or calm training to maintain mental engagement indoors.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Low-impact indoor enrichment provides routine variety during weather constraints.

raw_recommendations.name: Grooming and ear cleaning session
raw_recommendations.notes: Regular brushing and ear cleaning support coat and ear health for Golden Retrievers.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Essential grooming and ear care prevent health issues in large breed dogs.

raw_recommendations.name: Monthly veterinary check-in reminder
raw_recommendations.notes: Schedule or confirm annual or more frequent check-ups for senior dog preventive care.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Senior dogs require more frequent veterinary monitoring for joint and overall health.
